children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: a Jurisdiction
children.text: The district courts of the United States and all courts exercising general jurisdiction in the territories and possessions of the United States shall have jurisdiction in proceedings in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure to enjoin violations of this subchapter.

children.heading: Jury trial
children.label: b Jury trial
children.text: In case of an alleged violation of an injunction or restraining order issued under this section, trial shall, upon demand of the accused, be by a jury in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.heading: State cause of action pertaining to online pharmacies
children.label: c State cause of action pertaining to online pharmacies
A
children.text: to enjoin the conduct which violates this section;

children.label: B
children.text: to enforce compliance with this section;

children.label: C
children.text: to obtain damages, restitution, or other compensation, including civil penalties under ; andsection 842(b) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: to obtain such other legal or equitable relief as the court may find appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: Prior to filing a complaint under paragraph (1), the State shall serve a copy of the complaint upon the Attorney General and upon the United States Attorney for the judicial district in which the complaint is to be filed. In any case where such prior service is not feasible, the State shall serve the complaint on the Attorney General and the appropriate United States Attorney on the same day that the State’s complaint is filed in Federal district court of the United States. Such proceedings shall be independent of, and not in lieu of, criminal prosecutions or any other proceedings under this subchapter or any other laws of the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: Upon receiving notice respecting a civil action pursuant to this section, the United States shall have the right to intervene in such action and, upon so intervening, to be heard on all matters arising therein, and to file petitions for appeal.

children.label: C
children.text: Service of a State’s complaint on the United States as required in this paragraph shall be made in accord with the requirements of rule 4(i)(1) of the Federal Rule of Civil Procedure.1So in original. Probably should be “Rules”.1

children.heading: Powers conferred by State law
children.label: 3 Powers conferred by State law
children.text: For purposes of bringing any civil action under paragraph (1), nothing in this chapter shall prevent an attorney general of a State from exercising the powers conferred on the attorney general of a State by the laws of such State to conduct investigations or to administer oaths or affirmations or to compel the attendance of witnesses of or the production of documentary or other evidence.

children.heading: Venue
children.label: 4 Venue
children.text: Any civil action brought under paragraph (1) in a district court of the United States may be brought in the district in which the defendant is found, is an inhabitant, or transacts business or wherever venue is proper under . Process in such action may be served in any district in which the defendant is an inhabitant or in which the defendant may be found.section 1391 of title 28

children.heading: No private right of action
children.label: 5 No private right of action
children.text: No private right of action is created under this subsection.

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian Tribe or tribal organization, to the extent such tribe or tribal organization is lawfully carrying out a contract or compact under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act [ et seq.]; or25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: C
children.text: any employee of the United States or such Indian tribe or tribal organization, provided such agent or employee is acting in the usual course of business or employment, and within the scope of the official duties of such agent or employee therewith.
